name: West Indian Rice & Peas with Tempeh
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Dried black eyed peas (soaked for at least 5 hours)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, heat the 2 1/2 tablespoons of vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the uncooked brown rice and grated coconut, then sauté for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
2. Pour in the water and stir to combine. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low.
3. Cover the pot and simmer for about 40 minutes until all the liquid is absorbed and the rice is cooked through. Do not peek at the rice during this time.
4. In a separate pot or large skillet, cook the dried black eyed peas with the bay leaves in salted boiling water for 20-25 minutes, or until tender. Drain and set aside.
5. While the peas are cooking, heat the remaining 1/4 cup of vegetable oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and minced garlic, then sauté until the onions soften.
6. Stir in the sliced chile and bell pepper, then cook for an additional 2 minutes.
7. Add the fennel, cubed tempeh, salt, and pepper to the skillet. Cook, stirring frequently, until the tempeh is crisp and golden brown.
8. Once the tempeh is cooked, combine it with the rice mixture and stir until well combined.
9. Serve hot, garnished with chopped scallions if desired.
